summary: Sprint 1 Security Hardening Testing Complete. All 28 backend tests passed. Verified: 1) Health check returns 200, 2) All protected endpoints return 401 without auth cookie, 3) RBAC decorator (require_role) correctly implemented with admin wildcard access, 4) Audit trail includes tenant_id field, 5) JWT_SECRET removed from config.py and main.py (still in .env but unused), 6) All 88 route modules import without errors, 7) Frontend loads correctly showing login page with Google OAuth and Demo buttons.

minor.issue: JWT_SECRET still present in .env (line 5) but NOT used in code - legacy artifact, can be removed for cleanup

action_items: OPTIONAL: Remove JWT_SECRET from /app/backend/.env (line 5) - it's unused but still present as legacy artifact
critical_code_review_comments: RBAC decorator (core/rbac.py) correctly implements role-based access: admin always passes, other roles checked against allowed_roles list
Audit trail (services/audit_trail.py line 62) correctly includes tenant_id field from user dict
JWT_SECRET removed from config.py and main.py - cookie-based auth via Google OAuth is working
All 88 route files import successfully with RBAC applied to key routes (invoices, clients, backup)
Pydantic V1 validators deprecated warnings present - should migrate to V2 @field_validator in future

success_rate.frontend: 100% (Login page loads correctly with Google OAuth and Demo buttons)

seed_data_creation: None - tests use public endpoints and file inspection
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sprint 1 Security Hardening verified. Key findings: 1) RBAC applied to 76+ route files via require_role decorator. 2) Admin role has wildcard ['*'] access, guest has empty [] access. 3) Cookie-based auth via Google OAuth (no JWT decode in security.py). 4) Audit trail includes tenant_id at line 62. 5) JWT_SECRET still in .env but completely unused in code. 6) All 88 route modules import without errors. 7) Protected endpoints correctly return 401 without auth cookie.
